data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-f261d2a0">
    <title>cli: add 'ddx try <bead-id>' command — operator-targeted one-shot bead execution</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Operators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:
- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead
- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled
- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log

This blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.

ROOT CAUSE
'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.

Code today (relevant entry points):
- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:

  ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>]

Behavior:
1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.
2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.
3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):
   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.
   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.
   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.
4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).
5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).

NON-SCOPE
- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)
- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)
- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)
- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)
- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)

INTERSECTION
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.
- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.
- Refactor children Wave 2 dispatch will use 'ddx try' (codex harness) to deterministically dispatch each child in dep order.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/cmd/try.go exists implementing 'ddx try <bead-id>' as a cobra subcommand. Help text describes operator-targeted execution + the relationship to 'ddx work'.

2. 'ddx try unknown-bead-id' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead not found: unknown-bead-id'.

3. 'ddx try <closed-bead-id>' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead is not claimable (status=closed)'. Same shape for cancelled.

4. 'ddx try <bead-id-with-unmet-deps>' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead has unmet dependencies: <list>'.

5. 'ddx try <ready-bead-id>' claims the bead, executes via the existing execute-bead-loop machinery (one iteration, one bead), writes evidence to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/, and exits with the bead's terminal status code (0 = success/already_satisfied; 1 = preserved_for_review/no_changes; 2 = execution_failed).

6. Flag plumbing: --harness, --model, --profile, --provider, --effort, --opaque-passthrough, --no-review, --review-harness, --review-model. Each flag, when set, plumbs identically to the corresponding 'ddx work' flag (same struct fields in the runtime config).

7. WIRED-IN: the new 'ddx try' command is registered in cli/cmd/root.go (or wherever the cobra root command assembles subcommands). Verified by 'ddx try --help' output AND by 'git grep "tryCmd\|NewTryCommand" cli/cmd/' showing the registration.

8. Tests in cli/cmd/try_test.go:
   - TestTry_BeadNotFound — invalid ID returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit
   - TestTry_BeadClosed — closed bead returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit
   - TestTry_BeadUnmetDeps — bead with unclosed dep returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit
   - TestTry_HappyPath_ClaimsAndExecutes — fixture bead + stub executor; assert bead is claimed, attempted, and outcome reported (use the existing test fixtures from agent_execute_loop_test.go as a template)
   - TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough — set --harness=codex --model=gpt-5.4-mini and assert these reach the runtime config (capture via stub)

9. Quality gates:
   - cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/...
   - lefthook run pre-commit

10. Conventional commit message ending [<this-bead-id>]. Stage cli/cmd/try.go + cli/cmd/try_test.go + any cli/cmd/root.go registration line specifically; DO NOT use git add -A.

11. Manual verification (operator runs after merge): 'ddx try ddx-50da9674' (closed fixture-repo bead) returns non-zero with the closed-bead message; 'ddx try <some-ready-bead>' actually claims and executes it.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:cli, area:agent, kind:feature, reliability, bead-quality, wave:0</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="8179503c529b02c70d1ecb16b2f645fb0adafd6b" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260503T200645-f1713f26"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260503T200645-f1713f26/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260503T200645-f1713f26/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260503T200645-f1713f26/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260503T200645-f1713f26/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260503T200645-f1713f26/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Commit landed at `5ce92857`. Lefthook reported all checks ✔️ (the cmd-package install-test failures shown are pre-existing — they require a built `cli/build/ddx` binary and fail identically on baseline, as I verified with `git stash -u`; the lefthook script's exit code reflects only the last package iterated, which passes).

Summary of changes:
- **`cli/cmd/try.go`** — new `ddx try <bead-id>` command with pre-flight gates (not found, not claimable, unmet deps), full passthrough flag surface mirroring `ddx work`, and try-specific output/exit-code mapping (0 success/already_satisfied, 1 preserved/no_changes, 2 execution_failed).
- **`cli/cmd/try_test.go`** — covers AC #2/#3/#4/#5/#6/#7: not-found, closed, cancelled, unmet-deps, flag plumbing, registration, runtime-layer happy-path with target filtering, target-not-in-ready-set, and direct preflight unit tests.
- **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`** — `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.TargetBeadID` field; `nextCandidate` filters to that bead when set.
- **`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`** — `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl` takes `tryTargetBeadID`, forces `once=true` and wires `TargetBeadID`; on try mode delegates to `formatTryResult` for output + exit-code mapping.
- **`cli/cmd/work.go`** + **`cli/cmd/command_factory.go`** — pass empty target through and register the new command.
